descript.abstract: This dataset provides USFS Aquatic Organism Passage (AOP) survey data. It shows stream passage locations, passage measurements, and passability assessment categories from AOP field surveys. Structure included: culverts, dams, diversion dams, and natural features such as waterfalls.
descript.purpose: USFS Aquatic Organism Passage (AOP) survey data from the USFS Natural Resource Manager Aquatic Surveys database.

timeperd.current: Data are current as of publication date.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse
cntorgp.cntper: National Applications Liaison Office | US Forest Service (usda.gov)

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: This data is based on field gathered observations. No specific quality check or measures are available.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication.

procstep.procdesc: The source of this dataset is the Natural Resource Manager (NRM) database of the US Forest Service. The aquatic organism passage data are pulled from the oracle view, FS_NRIS_WATER.NRW_AOP_MEASURES_ASSESS_VW. This data is published using Feature Manipulation Engine Software. The spatial features were extracted from the transactional feature class fs_nris_wit.nris_wit_site_pt and published to the target feature class at EDW..

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: USFS Region

attrdomv.udom: Forest Service Region individual numbers or combination

attr.attrdef: National Forest ID

attrdomv.udom: Forest Service forest individual multi-digit number

attr.attrdef: National Forest Name
Name of a passage assessment survey, characterized for aquatic organism passage.
Name of a passage assessment survey, characterized for aquatic organism passage.
Protocol used to conduct a passage characterization survey
Last date a passage characterization survey was done on a particular location.
Year the last passage characterization survey was conducted
Latin name of species for which the passage was characterized.

attrdomv.udom: Latin names of species, no particular format

attr.attrdef: Common name of species for which the passage was characterized.

attrdomv.udom: Common names of speicies, no particular format

attr.attrdef: Life stage of the species for which the passage was characterized.

edom.edomvd: Sexually reproductive
The larval form, ammocoete
Males returning to spawn at early age and small size, also called grilse
Not mature, not sexually reproductive
Developing immature
Undergone metamorphosis, resembles an adult, sexual maturity is not clear.
Not Designated
Have a series of marks on their sides, parr marks
Postlarva, a period from yolk sac absorption to the juvenile stage, a subclass of larva
Prometamorphic, period of growth, especially limbs, minor metamorphic changes
no data entered

attr.attrdef: Assessment category of the culvert for aquatic organism passage.

edom.edomvd: Passage adequate for the analysis species based on local or regional screen that specifically addresses the needs of the analysis species.
Passage is inadequate for the analysis species based on local or regional screen that specifically addresses the needs of the analysis species.
Passage is indeterminate for the analysis species, based on local or regional criteria.
There is no suitable habitat upstream from passage.
Barrier constructed to prevent upstream migration of nonnative species
Passage resembles natural channel. Streambed substrate is continuous; particle size and arrangement are similar to natural channel. Bankfull channel width is not constricted.

attr.attrdef: Direction in which species passage was assessed.

edom.edomvd: Barrier prevents passage downstream..
Barrier prevents both upstream and downstream passage.
Barrier prevents passage upstream.
No value entered

attr.attrdef: Name of the location of a passage measure survey
Year the passage measure survey was conducted
Start date in month day year of the passage measure survey
Date of the sample in a passage measure survey
Name of a passage measure survey

attrdomv.udom: User assigned passage survey name, no specific format

attr.attrdef: Protocol used to conduct a passage measure survey

attrdomv.udom: User assigned passage protocol name, no specific format

attr.attrdef: Name of the sample in a passage measure survey

attrdomv.udom: User assigned passage sample name, no specific format

attr.attrdef: Type of Passage (culvert, chute, waterfall, etc.)

edom.edomvd: A structure spanning a waterway.
A short, steep drop in stream bed elevation often marked by boulders and agitated white water..
Chemical concentrations (natural or human caused) in the stream..

As applied to stream flow, the term CHUTE refers to a new route taken by a stream when its main flow is diverted to the inside of a bend, along a trough between low ridges formed by deposition on the inside of the bend where water velocities were reduced.
A transverse drain or waterway under a road, railroad, canal, or other obstruction.

A structure of earth, rock, or concrete designed to form a basin and hold water back to make a pond, lake, or reservoir. A barrier built, usually across a watercourse, for impounding or diverting the flow of water.
A dam created by a beaver.
Debris Dam of wood, rocks, soils.

A barrier built to divert part or all of the water from a stream into a different course. The diversion dam is commonly constructed on a natural river channel and is designed to check or elevate the water level for diversion into a main canal system. Also referred to as Diversion Cofferdam..
A reservoir or dam used in streams, canals and irrigation systems to reduce the mismatch between downstream demands and upstream water supplies in order to maintain a balanced operation.
Hydropower Dam.
Splash Dam
A constructed fill or wall which regulates water levels
An at-grade stream crossing where one can cross by walking, riding on an animal or in a vehicle using the bottom of the channel in lieu of a bridged or culverted crossing.
Traffic drives through the stream until increasing flows cause the water to become too deep to drive through.
Includes a low-flow conveyance structure such as a culvert, so that traffic does not travel through the water at low flows.
A huge mass of ice, formed on land by the compaction and recrystallization of snow, that moves very slowly downslope or outward due to its own weight.
Turbulent fast water
Water Temperature.
A waterfall or other precipitous descent of water.
A barrier placed in a channel to divert fish and other biota or water. A fence of stakes, brushwood, or the like, SET in a stream, tideway, or inlet of the sea, for taking fish.

attr.attrdef: Material of which a culvert is made

attrdomv.udom: Description of material used, no specific format

attr.attrdef: Passage is functional, impaired, or not functioning as designed

edom.edomvd: Human-Made structure is functioning as designed.
Human-Made structure is impaired, partially functional, but not as designed and may be in need of maintenance.
Human-Made structure is not functioning as designed.

attr.attrdef: Jumping distance as measured in the field in meters
Spill pool depth as measured in the field in meters
Structure height in meters
Average bankfull width as measured in the field in meters
Average bankfull width calculated by cross section in meters
Contains "UPSTREAM" or "DOWNSTREAM" or "UNKNOWN" indicating the direction in which observations were collected in a longitudinal profile.

edom.edomvd: observations were collected in downstream direction for longitudinal profile.
observation direction unknown.
observations were collected in upstream direction for longitudinal profile..
No data entered.

attr.attrdef: The slope in percent from P0-Upstrm_Pt to P1-Inlet_Grad_Ctrl_Pt

attrdomv.udom: numeric value expressed as a percentage, range 0-100

attr.attrdef: Approach gradient from P1-Inlet_Grad_Ctrl_Pt to P2-Clvrt_Inlet_Invert

attrdomv.udom: numeric value expressed as a percentage, range 0-100

attr.attrdef: The horizontal distance from P2-Clvrt_Inlet_Invert to P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert.
The slope in percent from P2-Clvrt_Inlet_Invert to P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert

attrdomv.udom: numeric value expressed as a percentage, range 0-100

attr.attrdef: The slope in decimal degrees from P2-Clvrt_Inlet_Invert to P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert

attrdomv.udom: numeric value expressed in degrees, range 0-90

attr.attrdef: The calculated value barrier_gradient_pct * barrier_length_m
The difference in elevation between the P2-Clvrt_Inlet_Invert and the P6-Outlet_Pl_Tlwtr_Ctrl.
The length between P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert and P5-Pool_Bottom.
The difference in elevation between the P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert and the water surface at P5-Pool_Bottom.
The difference in elevation between the P4-Clvrt_Outlet_Invert and P6-Outlet_Pl_Tlwtr_Ctrl.
The difference in elevation between the water surface at P5-Pool_Bottom and the bed at P5-Pool_Bottom.
The difference in elevation between P5-Pool_Bottom and P6-Outlet_Pl_Tlwtr_Ctrl.
The slope in percent from P6-Outlet_Pl_Tlwtr_Ctrl to P7-Dwnstrm_End.

attrdomv.udom: numeric value expressed as a percentage, range 0-100

attr.attrdef: Unique ID for Assess Spatial ID
Unique, computer created Survey ID for the characterization survey

attrdomv.udom: Pseudo random value used as foreign key in a relational database system

attr.attrdef: Unique, computer created Survey ID for a passage survey.

attrdomv.udom: Pseudo random value used as foreign key in a relational database system

attr.attrdef: Unique, computer created Survey ID for a passage sample.

attrdomv.udom: Pseudo random value used as foreign key in a relational database system

attr.attrdef: Unique, computer created Spatial ID for the survey location

attrdomv.udom: Pseudo random value used as foreign key in a relational database system

attr.attrdef: Unique, computer created Spatial ID for the sample location

attrdomv.udom: Pseudo random value used as foreign key in a relational database system

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
